guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Understanding / blank slate / first foothold

dossierState.situation: A founder has strong anecdotal demand for a new B2B analytics feature but no validated evidence about which customer segment would actually buy first.
dossierState.main_goal: Decide whether to invest in building the analytics feature this quarter.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: The user does not know what first move would reduce uncertainty fastest.

input.situation: A founder has strong anecdotal demand for a new B2B analytics feature but no validated evidence about which customer segment would actually buy first.
input.main_goal: Decide whether to invest in building the analytics feature this quarter.

situationRead: Frames the goal as a decision, not a general exploration
nextStep: Uses a decisive verb such as choose, define, capture, or validate
decisionPrompt: Should point toward one binary decision about which unknown to resolve first

rejectIf: Suggests broad market research with no concrete angle
Uses phrasing like "consider", "explore", or "start by reviewing"
Provides more than one equally weighted next step

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Understanding / stuck with scattered tasks

dossierState.situation: A team is investigating rising churn but has collected scattered notes from support, product, and sales without a clear pattern yet.
dossierState.main_goal: Identify the most likely cause of churn within one week.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: The user has tasks, but still does not know which one should become the focal point.

input.situation: A team is investigating rising churn but has collected scattered notes from support, product, and sales without a clear pattern yet.
input.main_goal: Identify the most likely cause of churn within one week.
input.user_input: I have a few tasks already but I still feel stuck.

situationRead: Acknowledges that the work is scattered and needs one focal proof point
nextStep: Locks onto one existing task explicitly
Explains why that task best reduces uncertainty
suggestedTasks: Support the chosen task instead of inventing a parallel investigation
decisionPrompt: Should narrow to a yes/no style choice about which evidence source matters most

rejectIf: Introduces a new unrelated research task
Keeps all three tasks equally active
Uses generic strategy language with no reference to the existing queue

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Understanding / too many tasks / needs ruthless focus

dossierState.situation: An operations lead is diagnosing delayed enterprise onboarding and has already listed many possible causes across sales handoff, provisioning, training, and compliance review.
dossierState.main_goal: Find the primary cause of onboarding delays before the next leadership review.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: The user has too many reasonable tasks and no obvious forcing function.

input.situation: An operations lead is diagnosing delayed enterprise onboarding and has already listed many possible causes across sales handoff, provisioning, training, and compliance review.
input.main_goal: Find the primary cause of onboarding delays before the next leadership review.
input.user_input: There are too many possible next moves. Which one matters most?

situationRead: Recognizes that task overload is the actual friction point
nextStep: Selects the one task most likely to isolate the primary delay source
suggestedTasks: Trim the work around the chosen task
Reduce analysis spread rather than expand it
decisionPrompt: Should frame a binary tradeoff, not an open brainstorm

rejectIf: Returns another long list of possibilities
Suggests doing several tasks in parallel
Fails to reduce scope

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Structuring / no tasks / turn thinking into plan

dossierState.situation: A product team has agreed on a pricing experiment direction but has not translated it into an execution plan.
dossierState.main_goal: Launch a controlled pricing test next month.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: The user knows the direction but has no operating structure yet.

input.situation: A product team has agreed on a pricing experiment direction but has not translated it into an execution plan.
input.main_goal: Launch a controlled pricing test next month.
input.user_input: How do I turn this into a real plan?

situationRead: States that structure, sequence, and ownership are now the central need
nextStep: Forces one planning move such as defining sequence, owner, or success criteria
suggestedTasks: Translate the plan into discrete execution-ready tasks
decisionPrompt: Should ask for one concrete structural choice, not broad planning

rejectIf: Falls back into discovery work
Suggests generic planning without ownership or order
Uses weak verbs like review or assess

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Structuring / tasks exist / unclear priority

dossierState.situation: A revops manager is building a lead-routing redesign and already has candidate tasks, but nobody agrees which dependency matters first.
dossierState.main_goal: Finalize a workable lead-routing rollout plan this week.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: The user has a plan skeleton but no forced sequence.

input.situation: A revops manager is building a lead-routing redesign and already has candidate tasks, but nobody agrees which dependency matters first.
input.main_goal: Finalize a workable lead-routing rollout plan this week.
input.user_input: Which task should drive the plan first?

situationRead: Identifies the missing forced order as the main blocker
nextStep: Chooses one existing task and makes it the sequencing anchor
suggestedTasks: Support the chosen task by clearing dependencies or defining success
decisionPrompt: Should resolve a concrete dependency question

rejectIf: Treats all tasks as equally important
Invents new work before sequencing the existing plan

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Structuring / momentum exists / keep plan tightening

dossierState.situation: A founder has already defined customer segments and pricing boundaries for a services offer and now needs to lock the operating model.
dossierState.main_goal: Finalize a launch-ready offer structure by Friday.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: The user has traction, but may drift back into polishing instead of locking decisions.

input.situation: A founder has already defined customer segments and pricing boundaries for a services offer and now needs to lock the operating model.
input.main_goal: Finalize a launch-ready offer structure by Friday.
input.user_input: We already made some progress. What should happen next?

situationRead: Recognizes that momentum exists and should be preserved
nextStep: Pushes a continuation step tied to the current task queue
suggestedTasks: Tighten the plan around the next unresolved dependency
decisionPrompt: Should sound like a forced choice that protects pace

rejectIf: Suggests returning to market exploration
Encourages more planning instead of closure
Ignores visible progress

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Action / tasks exist / no completed work yet

dossierState.situation: A team is launching a webinar funnel and has the work defined, but no execution has actually started.
dossierState.main_goal: Launch the webinar funnel before the campaign window closes.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: The team has a queue but still has not made the first visible move.

input.situation: A team is launching a webinar funnel and has the work defined, but no execution has actually started.
input.main_goal: Launch the webinar funnel before the campaign window closes.
input.user_input: We have tasks but still have not started. What now?

situationRead: Names the real issue as lack of visible execution
nextStep: Locks onto one existing task and pushes immediate completion or progress
suggestedTasks: Help the chosen task move faster instead of widening scope

rejectIf: Talks about planning instead of doing
Returns a generic "start executing" message with no object

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Action / momentum exists / keep moving

dossierState.situation: A hiring lead has already finished role definitions and candidate outreach, and interviews are now underway.
dossierState.main_goal: Close two senior hires this month.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: The user might reopen planning instead of pushing the next closing step.

input.situation: A hiring lead has already finished role definitions and candidate outreach, and interviews are now underway.
input.main_goal: Close two senior hires this month.
input.user_input: Momentum is good right now. What is the next move?

situationRead: Acknowledges active momentum and risk of losing pace
nextStep: Pushes continuation or completion, not more design work
suggestedTasks: Directly unblock or complete the chosen action task

rejectIf: Suggests more strategic reflection
Introduces new planning work unrelated to the closing sequence

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Action / too many tasks / execution clutter

dossierState.situation: A go-to-market lead is running a launch week with many open items across content, enablement, tracking, and partner communication.
dossierState.main_goal: Hit launch week without a critical gap.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: The user is in motion but surrounded by too many plausible next actions.

input.situation: A go-to-market lead is running a launch week with many open items across content, enablement, tracking, and partner communication.
input.main_goal: Hit launch week without a critical gap.
input.user_input: There are too many live tasks. Which one should we push right now?

situationRead: Recognizes that execution clutter, not uncertainty, is the main friction
nextStep: Selects one task that protects launch quality most directly
suggestedTasks: Support that exact task or remove blockers around it
decisionPrompt: Should ask a binary quality/risk tradeoff question

rejectIf: Returns a broad prioritization lecture
Suggests doing several tasks simultaneously
Fails to collapse the queue into one current action

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Understanding / unclear input / weak signal

dossierState.situation: A team believes something is wrong in enterprise expansion, but the user only knows that recent deals feel slower and less predictable.
dossierState.main_goal: Identify the first useful angle to investigate in enterprise slowdown.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: The input is vague, so the AI must still force one useful first move.

input.situation: Something feels off in enterprise expansion, but the signal is still fuzzy.
input.main_goal: Identify the first useful angle to investigate in enterprise slowdown.
input.user_input: I am not sure where to look yet.

situationRead: Acknowledges ambiguity without mirroring it back passively
nextStep: Forces one concrete first investigation angle
suggestedTasks: Support the chosen angle with specific evidence-gathering steps
decisionPrompt: Should collapse ambiguity into one binary starting choice

rejectIf: Repeats that more context is needed without proposing a concrete first move
Returns a vague exploration instruction

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Structuring / conflicting signals / multiple directions

dossierState.situation: A founder is choosing between a self-serve launch and a high-touch services offer. Both seem viable, but the team cannot structure the next quarter around both.
dossierState.main_goal: Choose one operating direction for the next quarter.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: Conflicting viable directions are blocking a usable operating plan.

input.situation: A founder is choosing between a self-serve launch and a high-touch services offer. Both seem viable, but the team cannot structure the next quarter around both.
input.main_goal: Choose one operating direction for the next quarter.
input.user_input: We have two real directions. How do we force the decision?

situationRead: Names conflicting directions as the real blocker
nextStep: Forces one comparison or constraint-based decision move
suggestedTasks: Support the forcing decision rather than elaborating both paths
decisionPrompt: Should frame a binary operating choice

rejectIf: Encourages advancing both directions in parallel
Expands planning instead of forcing the decision

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Action / blocked user / urgency

dossierState.situation: A launch manager is blocked because legal approval has not arrived, and the customer announcement must go out today.
dossierState.main_goal: Unblock the customer announcement before end of day.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: Urgency is high, but the next move must remove the actual blocker instead of spreading effort.

input.situation: A launch manager is blocked because legal approval has not arrived, and the customer announcement must go out today.
input.main_goal: Unblock the customer announcement before end of day.
input.user_input: We are blocked and the deadline is today. What do we do now?

situationRead: Names the blocker and urgency explicitly
nextStep: Targets the blocker first, not secondary work
decisionPrompt: Should force the fastest unblock choice

rejectIf: Suggests polishing secondary assets before removing the blocker
Ignores the same-day urgency

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Action / risk-sensitive / caution outranks speed

dossierState.situation: A compliance lead is preparing a customer data migration with time pressure, but one missing privacy check could create legal exposure.
dossierState.main_goal: Complete the migration safely without creating compliance risk.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: The team is moving, but safety and compliance should outrank pure speed.

input.situation: A compliance lead is preparing a customer data migration with time pressure, but one missing privacy check could create legal exposure.
input.main_goal: Complete the migration safely without creating compliance risk.
input.user_input: We need to move quickly, but we cannot create risk.

situationRead: Acknowledges that caution should outrank raw speed
nextStep: Names the highest-risk control step first
suggestedTasks: Support safe execution rather than faster execution alone
decisionPrompt: Should sound like a safety-first forced choice

rejectIf: Optimizes for speed while skipping the risk-control step
Fails to mention compliance or verification

guidanceQualityScenarios.title: Structuring / overloaded plan / too many viable branches

dossierState.situation: A PMM lead is building a launch plan with too many parallel workstreams across pricing, messaging, sales enablement, and onboarding updates.
dossierState.main_goal: Reduce the launch plan to one workable execution sequence.
dossierState.hesitationMoment: There are too many viable plan branches, so ownership and sequence are still blurry.

input.situation: A PMM lead is building a launch plan with too many parallel workstreams across pricing, messaging, sales enablement, and onboarding updates.
input.main_goal: Reduce the launch plan to one workable execution sequence.
input.user_input: The plan keeps spreading. What should become the anchor now?

situationRead: Recognizes plan sprawl as the current problem
nextStep: Chooses one anchoring task for sequence and ownership
suggestedTasks: Support narrowing and ordering the plan

rejectIf: Expands the list of active workstreams
Fails to reduce the plan to one anchor

strongOutputPatterns: Uses one directive sentence for next_step, with one action only.
References an existing task explicitly when the queue already contains the right move.
Uses strong verbs such as define, choose, complete, write, capture, remove, send, or confirm.
Keeps suggested_tasks as supporting moves, not alternate strategies.
Preserves momentum by pushing continuation when progress already exists.
commonFailurePatterns: Multiple competing next steps in the same response.
Weak verbs such as review, assess, consider, or explore without concrete object and outcome.
Suggested tasks that duplicate next_step or drift into a new workstream.
Open-ended guidance that leaves the prioritization burden on the user.
Planning-oriented guidance in Action phase after momentum is already visible.
